commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0fa3a49e2d38583e81dcf6c80b58733cafc4f8a5)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a configurable `truncation-risk-latest` mode to bulk ClawScan rescans across the backend, admin API, moderation CLI, schemas, tests, docs, and moderation specification.

Reproducibility: yes. for the patch defect: source inspection establishes that eligibility uses stored bytes while scanner truncation uses prepared-content characters, so divergent target sets are possible without executing the branch.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers must approve the permanent security coverage predicate, while the contributor must resolve the conflict and supply real deployment proof.

target.title: Add truncation-risk ClawScan rescan mode
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds an operator rescan mode for active latest skills that are large enough to be affected by prompt truncation boundaries.

## Scenario this protects

If maintainers change scanner rules, adjust prompt limits, or identify a class of artifacts that needs fresh review, they need a focused way to queue rescans. Without that, the choices are either manual one-by-one rescans or a broad rescan of every active skill.

This PR adds the narrower path for the truncation-risk case.

## What changed

- Adds `truncation-risk-latest` to the admin bulk ClawScan rescan API.
- Adds the same mode to `clawhub-mod skills rescan-all`.
- Supports dry-run output so operators can count and sample the affected set before creating jobs.
- Queues fresh ClawScan jobs only for active latest skills whose primary `SKILL.md` or `skills.md` is large enough to deserve another look.
- Updates public/operator docs and the security moderation spec to describe scan results as current risk signals, not permanent certification.

## Operator flow

First inspect scope:

```sh
bun run mod -- skills rescan-all --mode truncation-risk-latest --dry-run --json
```

Then queue jobs when the scope looks right:

```sh
bun run mod -- skills rescan-all --mode truncation-risk-latest
```

## What this does not change

This does not change scanner verdict logic, install gating, hide/unhide behavior, or manual moderation decisions. It only adds a targeted way to refresh existing published artifacts.

## Validation

- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run convex/securityScan.test.ts`
- `(cd packages/clawhub-mod && ../../node_modules/.bin/vitest run -c vitest.config.ts src/commands/moderation.test.ts)`
- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run packages/schema/src/schemas.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/tsc -p packages/clawhub-mod/tsconfig.json --noEmit && ./node_modules/.bin/tsc -p packages/schema/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `bun run --cwd packages/schema build`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check convex/securityScan.ts convex/securityScan.test.ts convex/httpApiV1/skillsV1.ts packages/clawhub-mod/src/cli.ts packages/clawhub-mod/src/commands/moderation.ts packages/clawhub-mod/src/commands/moderation.test.ts packages/schema/src/schemas.ts packages/clawhub/src/schema/schemas.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --type-aware --tsconfig ./tsconfig.oxlint.json convex/securityScan.ts convex/securityScan.test.ts convex/httpApiV1/skillsV1.ts packages/clawhub-mod/src/cli.ts packages/clawhub-mod/src/commands/moderation.ts packages/clawhub-mod/src/commands/moderation.test.ts packages/schema/src/schemas.ts packages/clawhub/src/schema/schemas.ts`
- `git diff --check`
